# validate.tract_boundary_error { #tslai.validate.tract_boundary_error }

```python
tslai.validate.tract_boundary_error(tracks, truth_states, sample)
```

Tract-boundary localization error against the truth.

For each true ancestry switch, measures the distance to the nearest inferred
switch (from the posterior ``argmax``).

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**tracks**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[int](`int`), [list](`list`)\[[Segment](`tslai.output.Segment`)\]\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Posterior segment tracks per sample.

<code>[**truth_states**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[int](`int`), [list](`list`)\[[tuple](`tuple`)\[[float](`float`), [float](`float`), [int](`int`)\]\]\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   True ancestry-state tracts per sample (e.g. from :func:`map_truth`).

<code>[**sample**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Sample id to score.

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Keys ``"n_true_switches"`` (number of true switch points), ``"median_error"`` and ``"mean_error"`` (median/mean distance from each true switch to the nearest inferred switch). The errors are ``nan`` when there are no true switches and ``inf`` when there are no inferred ones.
